back_translation: When He had spoken all the words for the people to hear completely, He entered the city of Capernaum.
There was a centurion, and a servant of his whom he loved dearly was gravely ill and near death.
When the centurion heard reports about Jesus, he sent some elders of the Jews to invite Him to come and heal his servant.
They came to Jesus and earnestly pleaded with Him, saying, "This man deserves that You would do this for him,
for he loves our nation, and he himself built the synagogue for us."

So Jesus went with them. When He was not yet far from the house, the centurion sent friends to tell Him, "Lord, please do not trouble Yourself, for I am not worthy to have You come under the roof of my house.
That is why I did not consider myself worthy even to come to You. But just speak a single word, and my servant will be healed.
For I myself am under authority, and I have soldiers under my command. I say to one, 'Go,' and he goes; I say to another, 'Come,' and he comes; and I say to my slave, 'Do this,' and he does it."
When Jesus heard these words, He marveled at the man. Turning to the crowd that was following Him, He said, "I tell you, even in Israel I have never found such great faith."
When those who had been sent returned to the house, they found that the servant had already recovered.
Soon afterward, He went to a city called Nain. His disciples and a large crowd journeyed with Him.
As He drew near the gate of the city, behold, a dead man was being carried out. He was the only son of his mother, who was a widow, and a large crowd from the city was with her.
When the Lord saw her, He was filled with compassion for her, and said to her, "Do not weep."
Then He came close and touched the bier, and those carrying it stopped. He said, "Young man, I tell you, arise!"
The one who had been dead sat up and began to speak, and He gave him back to his mother.
Fear fell upon everyone, and they praised God, saying, "A great prophet has risen among us," and, "God has come to visit His people."
The news about Him spread throughout the region of Judea and all the surrounding area.
John's disciples reported all these things to him. So John summoned two of his disciples,
and he sent them to inquire of the Lord, saying, "Are You the One who is to come, or should we wait for another?"
When these men came to Him, they said, "John the Baptizer sent us to You, asking, 'Are You the One who is to come, or should we wait for another?'"
At that very time, He healed many people of diseases, afflictions, and evil spirits, and He gave sight to many blind people so they could see again.

He answered the two men, "Go and report to John what you have seen and heard: the blind see again, the lame walk, lepers are made clean, the deaf hear, the dead are raised, and the poor receive the gospel proclaimed to them.
And whoever does not stumble because of Me is blessed."
When John's men had departed, He began to speak to the crowds about John: "What did you go out into the wilderness to see? A reed swaying in the wind?
Or what did you go out to see? A man dressed in soft clothing? Behold, those who wear fine clothing and live in luxury are in royal palaces.
But what did you go out to see? A prophet? Yes, I tell you, and even more than a prophet.
This John is the one about whom it is written: 'Behold, I send My messenger ahead of You, who will prepare Your way before You.'
I tell you, among those born of women there is no one greater than John; yet even the smallest in the kingdom of God is greater than John."
All the people who heard, including the tax collectors, declared that God was righteous, because they had received John's baptism.
But the Pharisees and experts in the Law rejected God's purpose for themselves, because they would not receive baptism from John.
"Therefore, to what shall I compare the people of this generation? What are they like?
They are like children sitting in the marketplace calling to one another: 'We piped for you to hear, but you would not dance; we sang a dirge, but you would not weep.'
For John the Baptizer came not eating food and not drinking wine, and you say, 'He is demon-possessed.'
The Son of Man came eating and drinking, and you say, 'Look, this man is a glutton and a drunkard, a friend of tax collectors and sinners.'
But wisdom is declared righteous by all her own children."
A certain Pharisee invited Him to dine with him, so He entered the house of that Pharisee and reclined at the table.
Behold, there was a woman in the city who was a sinner. When she learned that He was reclining to dine in the Pharisee's house, she brought an alabaster jar containing perfumed oil,
and she stood weeping behind Him at His feet. Her tears began to fall upon His feet, and she wiped His feet with her own hair, kissed His feet, and anointed them with the perfumed oil.

When the Pharisee who had invited Him saw this, he thought to himself, "If this man were truly a prophet, he would know who the woman touching him is and what kind of woman she is — for she is a sinner."
Jesus answered him, "Simon, I have something to say to you." Simon said, "Teacher, please speak."
"Two debtors owed a certain moneylender. One owed five hundred denarii, and the other fifty.
When both had nothing with which to repay, he canceled the debts of both. So, of the two, which one will love him more?"
Simon answered, "I suppose it is the one to whom he forgave more." He said to him, "You have judged correctly."

Then He turned toward the woman and said to Simon, "Do you see this woman? When I came into your house, you did not bring Me water to wash My feet; but this woman has cleansed My feet with her tears and wiped them with her hair.
You did not greet Me with a kiss, but from the time I entered, this woman has not stopped kissing My feet.
You did not anoint My head with oil, but this woman has anointed My feet with perfumed oil.
Therefore I tell you, her many sins have been forgiven, for she has loved much. But the one who has been forgiven little, loves little."
He said to her, "Your sins are forgiven."
Those who were reclining at the table with Him began to say among themselves, "Who is this who can even forgive sins?"
He said to the woman, "Your faith has saved you; go in peace."
